session.user:
let's plan next performance win for
Resolving branch and plan context... Checking for existing PR... Phase 1: Graphite Submit Auto-forcing: plan implementation branch Running gt submit... Running in non-interactive mode. Inline prompts to fill PR fields will be skipped.
🥞 Validating that this Graphite stack is ready to submit...
✏️ Preparing to submit PRs for the following branches... ▸ plnd/parallelize-diff-fetch-03-05-1702 (Update code)
🚀 Pushing branches to remote...
📨 Creating/updating PRs... plnd/parallelize-diff-fetch-03-05-1702: https://app.graphite.com/github/pr/dagster-io/erk/8795 (updated) WARNING: Branch plnd/parallelize-diff-fetch-03-05-1702 has been updated remotely. Force submitting local version to remote... Graphite submit completed
Getting PR info... PR #8795 ready
Phase 2: Getting diff and plan context Incorporating plan #8795
Phase 3: Generating PR description Reading diff file... Diff loaded (10,837 chars) Analyzing changes with Claude... Still waiting... (3s) Still waiting... (6s) Still waiting... (9s) Still waiting... (12s) Still waiting... (15s) PR description generated
Phase 5: Updating PR metadata PR metadata updated
Waiting for PR to appear in Graphite cache... ✅ PR submitted
Action: Created PR #8795 Branch: plnd/parallelize-diff-fetch-03-05-1702 → master Title: Parallelize extract_diff and fetch_plan_context in PR submit pipeline PR: https://github.com/dagster-io/erk/pull/8795 Graphite: https://app.graphite.com/github/pr/dagster-io/erk/8795 ➜ erk-slot-03 git:(plnd/parallelize-diff-fetch-03-05-1702)
instead of invoking claude code cli have Phase 3: Generating PR description Reading diff file... Diff loaded (10,837 chars) Analyzing changes with Claude... Still waiting... (3s) Still waiting... (6s) Still waiting... (9s) Still waiting... (12s) Still waiting... (15s) PR description generated instead use the fast prompt llm caller to use the api
session.assistant: Let me explore the PR submit pipeline and the fast prompt LLM caller.

tool_use.name: Agent
tool_use.param: FIRST check docs/learned/index.md for existing documentation on this topic. Read relevant ...[truncated 546 chars]...d .claude/ directories. Be thorough - trace the full code path for description generation.

session.tool_result:
Perfect! Now I have a comprehensive understanding. Let me create a summary document for the user:
## Summary: PR Submit Pipeline Phase 3 Analysis
I've traced the complete code path for Phase 3 "Generating PR description" in the PR submit pipeline. Here's what I found:
### 1. **Main PR Submit Pipeline Entry Point** **File:** `/Users/schrockn/.erk/repos/erk/worktrees/erk-slot-03/src/erk/cli/commands/pr/submit_pipeline.py`
The orchestration is managed by the `_submit_pipeline()` function (line 1003-1015) which executes these 10 steps: 1. `prepare_state` - Discover branch/plan context 2. `cleanup_impl_for_submit` - Remove staging dirs 3. `commit_wip` - Commit uncommitted changes 4. `capture_existing_pr_body` - Capture PR body before gt submit 5. `push_and_create_pr` - Create/find PR (Graphite-first or core flow) 6. `extract_diff_and_fetch_plan_context` - **Parallelize Phase 2** (concurrent extraction of diff and plan) 7. `generate_description` - **Phase 3: Generate PR description** 8. `enhance_with_graphite` - Graphite optimization 9. `finalize_pr` - Update PR metadata 10. `link_pr_to_objective_nodes` - Link to objective roadmap
### 2. **Phase 3: generate_description() Function** **Location:** submit_pipeline.py, lines 638-681
```python def generate_description(ctx: ErkContext, state: SubmitState) -> SubmitState | SubmitError: """Generate AI PR title and body via CommitMessageGenerator.""" if state.skip_description: return state click.echo(click.style("Phase 3: Generating PR description", bold=True))
... [114 lines omitted] ...
- Returns `PromptResult(success: bool, output: str, error: str | None)` - Each step: `(ErkContext, SubmitState) -> SubmitState | SubmitError`
session.assistant: Good, I have a clear picture. The current PR description generation calls `claude` CLI via subprocess (`claude --print --model haiku ...`), which has significant startup overhead. Let me find what direct API callers exist.

34→ return LlmCallFailed(message="Unexpected response type from LLM") 36→ except APIError as exc: 37→ logger.warning("LLM call failed: %s", exc) 38→ return LlmCallFailed(message=str(exc))

32→ ProcessErrorEvent, 53→def format_prompt_error( 59→ """Format error message for failed prompt execution. 61→ Constructs a structured error message from subprocess failure details. 65→ stderr: Standard error output 69→ Formatted error string like "Exit code 1 | stderr: ... | stdout: ..." 71→ error_parts = [f"Exit code {returncode}"] 73→ error_parts.append(f"stderr: {stderr.strip()}") 76→ error_parts.append(f"stdout: {stdout_preview}") 77→ return " | ".join(error_parts) 153→ error_msg = f"Claude command {command} failed with exit code {result.returncode}" 154→ yield ErrorEvent(message=error_msg) 163→ # Handle Popen errors (e.g., claude not found, permission denied) 174→ except OSError as e: 175→ yield ProcessErrorEvent( 176→ message=f"Failed to start Claude CLI: {e}
Command: {' '.join(cmd_args)}" 282→ diag += "

 Exit code: {returncode}" 325→ error_msg += f"
 Lines processed: {line_count}" 327→ error_msg += "
 Stderr:
" + "".join(stderr_output).strip() 328→ yield ErrorEvent(message=error_msg) 364→ # Parse JSON safely - JSON parsing requires exception handling 371→ except json.JSONDecodeError: 386→ "is_error": None, 456→ result["is_error"] = data.get("is_error", False) 490→ raise RuntimeError("Claude CLI not found

1→"""Branch slug generation via LLM inference. 2→ 3→This module provides branch name slug generation using a fast LLM call 4→to distill plan titles into meaningful 2-4 word slugs before they 5→enter the branch naming pipeline. 6→ 7→Follows the CommitMessageGenerator pattern from src/erk/core/commit_message_generator.py. 8→""" 9→ 10→from __future__ import annotations 11→ 12→from dataclasses import dataclass 13→ 14→from erk_shared.core.prompt_executor import PromptExecutor 15→from erk_shared.naming import sanitize_worktree_name 16→ 17→BRANCH_SLUG_SYSTEM_PROMPT = """\ 18→You are a branch name slug generator. Given a title, return ONLY a concise 19→2-4 word slug using lowercase letters and hyphens. No explanation. 20→ 21→Rules: 22→- Use 2-4 hyphenated words (e.g., "fix-auth-session", "add-plan-validation") 23→- Capture the distinctive essence, not generic words 24→- Drop filler words: "the", "a", "for", "and", "implementation", "plan" 25→- Prefer verbs: add, fix, refactor, update, consolidate, extract, migrate 26→- Never exceed 30 characters total 27→- Output ONLY the slug, nothing else""" 28→ 29→ 30→@dataclass(frozen=True)

PreToolUse:ExitPlanMode hook error: [command -v erk >/dev/null 2>&1 || exit 0; ERK_HOOK_ID=exit-plan-mode-hook erk exec exit-plan-mode-hook]: DISPLAY PLAN: Before asking the question below, read the plan file and display its contents to the user with proper markdown formatting: /Users/schrockn/.claude/plans/dazzling-conjuring-hare.md
PLAN SAVE PROMPT
A plan exists for this session but has not been saved.
Use AskUserQuestion to ask the user: question: "📋 Replace Claude CLI subprocess with direct Anthropic API call for PR description generation Current context: (wt:erk-slot-03) (br:plnd/parallelize-diff-fetch-03-05-1702) (pr:#8795)\n\nWhat would you like to do with this plan?" header: "br:plnd/para"
IMPORTANT: Present options in this exact order: 1. "Create new branch and planned PR. Stays on current branch." - Create a new branch, save plan as draft PR. 2. "Implement without saving" - Implement directly on the current branch without saving a plan PR. 3. "View/Edit the Plan" - Open plan in editor to review or modify before deciding.
If user chooses 'Create new branch and planned PR': 1. Run /erk:plan-save 2. Call ExitPlanMode to end the planning session.
If user chooses 'Implement without saving': 1. Create implement-now marker: erk exec marker create --session-id a94fe7e5-7102-4d65-82a9-b87e8f3d9191 \ exit-plan-mode-hook.implement-now 2. Call ExitPlanMode 3. After exiting plan mode, implement the changes directly on the current branch. Do NOT run 'erk exec setup-impl' or create a new branch. Read the plan from: /Users/schrockn/.claude/plans/dazzling-conjuring-hare.md Implement changes, run CI, and optionally 'erk pr submit' when done.
... [6 lines omitted] ...

1→# Replace Claude CLI subprocess with direct Anthropic API call for PR description generation 2→ 3→## Context 4→ 5→PR description generation (Phase 3 of `pr-submit`) currently spawns a `claude --print --model haiku` subprocess via `PromptExecutor.execute_prompt()`. This takes 15+ seconds due to Node.js startup + Claude Code initialization overhead. The codebase already has `AnthropicLlmCaller` (`src/erk/core/fast_llm.py`) that makes direct Anthropic SDK calls — currently used only for slug generation with `max_tokens=50`. We should use this fast path for PR description generation too. 6→ 7→## Changes 8→ 9→### 1. Extend `LlmCaller` ABC with `max_tokens` parameter 10→ 11→**File:** `packages/erk-shared/src/erk_shared/core/llm_caller.py` 12→ 13→- Add `max_tokens: int` keyword parameter to `LlmCaller.call()` 14→ 15→### 2. Update `AnthropicLlmCaller` to accept `max_tokens` 16→ 17→**File:** `src/erk/core/fast_llm.py` 18→ 19→- Pass `max_tokens` through to `client.messages.create()` 20→- Remove hardcoded `max_tokens=50` 21→ 22→### 3. Update `FakeLlmCaller` 23→ 24→**File:** `packages/erk-shared/src/erk_shared/core/fakes.py` 25→ 26→- Add `max_tokens` parameter to `call()` signature 27→ 28→### 4. Update all existing callers of `LlmCaller.call()` 29→ 30→Search for all `.call(` invocations on `LlmCaller` instances and add `max_tokens=50` to preserve existing behavior.
... [59 lines omitted] ...
38→- Map `LlmResponse` → success, `NoApiKey | LlmCallFailed` → error result

tool_use.name: AskUserQuestion

session.tool_result:
User has answered your questions: "📋 Replace Claude CLI subprocess with direct Anthropic API call for PR description generation Current context: (wt:erk-slot-03) (br:plnd/parallelize-diff-fetch-03-05-1702) (pr:#8795)
What would you like to do with this plan?"="Create new branch and planned PR". You can now continue with the user's answers in mind.
Launching skill: erk:plan-save
session.user:
# /erk:plan-save
Save the current session's plan to GitHub with session context.
## Usage
```bash /erk:plan-save # Standalone plan /erk:plan-save --plan-type=learn # Learn plan (erk-learn label) /erk:plan-save --objective=123 # Explicit objective link (overrides marker) /erk:plan-save --current-branch # Use current branch instead of creating new plnd/ branch ```
Objective linking: if a plan was created via `/erk:objective-plan`, the session's objective-context marker is read automatically by the save command. Use `--objective=<number>` to override the marker or provide the link when no marker exists (e.g., during replan).
## Plan Storage
The plan is saved using the configured backend:
- **Draft PR backend** (`PLAN_BACKEND = "draft_pr"`): Creates a branch, pushes a plan commit, and opens a draft PR. Plan content is in the PR body after the metadata separator. The JSON output contract includes `plan_number`, `plan_url`, `title`, `branch_name`, `plan_backend`.
## Agent Instructions
### Step 1: Parse Arguments
Check `` for the `--plan-type`, `--objective`, and `--current-branch` flags:
``` If contains "--plan-type=<type>": - Extract the type (standard or learn) - Store as PLAN_TYPE variable - Set PLAN_TYPE_FLAG to "--plan-type=<type>" Else: - Set PLAN_TYPE_FLAG to empty string
If contains "--objective=<number>": - Extract the number - Set OBJECTIVE_FLAG to "--objective=<number>" Else: - Set OBJECTIVE_FLAG to empty string
If contains "--current-branch": - Set CURRENT_BRANCH_FLAG to "--current-branch" Else: - Set CURRENT_BRANCH_FLAG to empty string ```
### Step 2: Generate Branch Slug
**Skip this step if `--current-branch` was passed.** The current branch is used directly.
Before saving, generate a branch slug from the plan title. Read the plan title from the plan file you wrote in plan mode (the first `# ` heading).
Generate a branch slug from the title:
- 2-4 hyphenated lowercase words, max 30 characters - Capture distinctive essence, drop filler words (the, a, for, implementation, plan) - Prefer action verbs: add, fix, refactor, update, consolidate, extract, migrate - Examples: "fix-auth-session", "add-plan-validation", "refactor-gateway-abc"
Store the result as `BRANCH_SLUG`.
### Step 3: Generate Plan Summary
Write a concise 2-3 sentence summary of the plan. This summary will be visible at the top of the PR description (above the collapsed full plan).
Guidelines:
- Focus on WHAT the plan does and WHY - Do not repeat the title - Plain text, no markdown headers or formatting - Avoid special shell characters (backticks, dollar signs) - Store as PLAN_SUMMARY
### Step 4: Run Save Command
Run this command with the session ID, summary, and optional flags.
If CURRENT_BRANCH_FLAG is set, pass `--current-branch` (no branch slug needed):
```bash erk exec plan-save --format json --session-id="a94fe7e5-7102-4d65-82a9-b87e8f3d9191" --current-branch --summary="${PLAN_SUMMARY}" ${PLAN_TYPE_FLAG} ${OBJECTIVE_FLAG} ```
Otherwise, pass the branch slug:
```bash erk exec plan-save --format json --session-id="a94fe7e5-7102-4d65-82a9-b87e8f3d9191" --branch-slug="${BRANCH_SLUG}" --summary="${PLAN_SUMMARY}" ${PLAN_TYPE_FLAG} ${OBJECTIVE_FLAG} ```
Parse the JSON output to extract `plan_number` for verification in Step 5.
If the command fails, display the error and stop.
### Step 5: Verify Objective Link (if applicable)
**Only run this step if `objective_issue` is non-null in the JSON output from Step 4.**
Verify the objective link was saved correctly:
```bash erk exec get-plan-metadata <plan_number> objective_issue ```
Parse the JSON response:
- If `success: true` and `value` matches the expected objective number: verification passed - If `success: false` or value doesn't match: verification failed
**On verification success:**
Display: `Verified objective link: #<objective-number>`
**On verification failure:**
Display error and remediation steps:
``` ERROR: Objective link verification failed Expected objective: #<expected> Actual: <actual-or-null>
The plan was saved but without the correct objective link. Fix: Close draft PR #<plan_number>, ensure the objective-context marker exists, and re-run /erk:plan-save. ```
Exit without creating the plan-saved marker. The session continues so the user can retry.
### Step 6: Update Objective Roadmap (if objective linked)
**Only run this step if `objective_issue` was non-null in JSON output and verification passed.**
Update the objective's roadmap table to show that a plan has been created for this node:
1. **Read the roadmap node marker** to get the node ID:
```bash step_id=$(erk exec marker read --session-id "a94fe7e5-7102-4d65-82a9-b87e8f3d9191" roadmap-step) ```
If the marker doesn't exist (command fails), skip this step - the plan wasn't created via `objective-plan`.
2. **Update the roadmap table** using the dedicated command:
```bash erk exec update-objective-node <objective-issue> --node "$step_id" --pr "#<plan_number>" --status in_progress ```
This atomically fetches the objective body, finds the matching node row, sets the Status cell to `in-progress`, and writes the updated body back.
3. **Report the update:**
Display: `Updated objective #<objective-issue> roadmap: node <step_id> → plan #<plan_number>`
**Error handling:** If the roadmap update fails, warn but continue - the plan was saved successfully, just the roadmap tracking didn't update. The user can manually update the objective.
### Step 7: Display Results
**If JSON contains `skipped_duplicate: true`:**
Display: `Plan already saved as #<plan_number> (duplicate skipped)`
Then call ExitPlanMode. The exit-plan-mode hook will present "what next?" options.
**Otherwise, on success**, display:
``` Plan "<title>" saved as draft PR #<plan_number> URL: <issue_url> ```
If objective was verified, also display: `Verified objective link: #<objective-number>`
Then call ExitPlanMode. The exit-plan-mode hook will present "what next?" options (implement in current worktree, implement in new worktree, or done).
On failure, display the error message and suggest:
- Checking that a plan exists (enter Plan mode and exit it first) - Verifying GitHub CLI authentication (`gh auth status`) - Checking network connectivity
### Step 5: Push Planning Session
After saving successfully, push the planning session for cross-machine learning. This captures the in-progress session state so it's available even if implementation happens on a different machine.
Run `erk exec capture-session-info` to get the session file path, then push it:
```bash erk exec upload-impl-session --session-id "a94fe7e5-7102-4d65-82a9-b87e8f3d9191" 2>/dev/null || true ```
If the plan was saved successfully and `plan_number` is known, use push-session directly for better stage tracking:
```bash erk exec push-session \ --session-file "<session_file_path>" \ --session-id "a94fe7e5-7102-4d65-82a9-b87e8f3d9191" \ --stage planning \ --source local \ --plan-id <plan_number> \ 2>/dev/null || true ```
Where `<session_file_path>` is obtained from `erk exec capture-session-info` output and `<plan_number>` is from Step 2's JSON output.
**Note:** This is non-critical. If it fails, the plan was still saved successfully. The `2>/dev/null || true` ensures graceful degradation.
## Session Tracking
After successfully saving a plan, the plan number is stored in a marker file that enables automatic plan updates in the same session.
**To read the saved plan number:**
```bash erk exec marker read --session-id "a94fe7e5-7102-4d65-82a9-b87e8f3d9191" plan-saved-issue ```
This returns the plan number (exit code 0) or exits with code 1 if no plan was saved in this session.
session.assistant:
I'll save the plan as a draft PR. Let me work through the steps.
**Branch slug:** `fast-api-pr-description`
**Plan summary:** Switches PR description generation from spawning a Claude CLI subprocess to using the existing AnthropicLlmCaller for direct Anthropic SDK API calls, reducing Phase 3 latency from 15+ seconds to 2-3 seconds. Extends the LlmCaller ABC with a max_tokens parameter and updates CommitMessageGenerator to use LlmCaller instead of PromptExecutor.

tool_use.name: Bash
tool_use.param: erk exec plan-save --format json --session-id="a94fe7e5-7102-4d65-82a9-b87e8f3d9191" --bra...[truncated 277 chars]... parameter and updates CommitMessageGenerator to use LlmCaller instead of PromptExecutor."
Save plan as draft PR via erk exec plan-save

session.assistant: Plan saved. Now pushing the planning session.

tool_use.name: Bash
tool_use.param: Push planning session for cross-machine learning

session.tool_result: {"uploaded": false, "reason": "no_impl_folder"}

Plan saved as draft PR #8801.

tool_use.name: ExitPlanMode
tool_use.param: # Replace Claude CLI subprocess with direct Anthropic API call for PR description generati...[truncated 4412 chars]...pr submit` on a branch with changes and verify Phase 3 completes in ~2-3s instead of 15+s

session.tool_result:
PreToolUse:ExitPlanMode hook error: [command -v erk >/dev/null 2>&1 || exit 0; ERK_HOOK_ID=exit-plan-mode-hook erk exec exit-plan-mode-hook]: Plan #8801 saved successfully.
Display ALL of the following next-steps commands to the user as plain text. Show every line exactly as written — do NOT summarize, truncate, or omit any lines. (do NOT use AskUserQuestion — just display the text):
Implement plan #8801: In current wt: source "$(erk br co --for-plan 8801 --script)" && erk implement (dangerously): source "$(erk br co --for-plan 8801 --script)" && erk implement -d In new wt: source "$(erk br co --new-slot --for-plan 8801 --script)" && erk implement (dangerously): source "$(erk br co --new-slot --for-plan 8801 --script)" && erk implement -d
Checkout plan #8801: In current wt: erk br co --for-plan 8801 In new wt: erk br co --new-slot --for-plan 8801
Dispatch plan #8801: CLI command: erk pr dispatch 8801 Slash command: /erk:pr-dispatch
Session complete. Do NOT call ExitPlanMode again.
session.assistant:
Plan #8801 saved successfully.
**Implement plan #8801:** - In current wt: `source "$(erk br co --for-plan 8801 --script)" && erk implement` - (dangerously): `source "$(erk br co --for-plan 8801 --script)" && erk implement -d` - In new wt: `source "$(erk br co --new-slot --for-plan 8801 --script)" && erk implement` - (dangerously): `source "$(erk br co --new-slot --for-plan 8801 --script)" && erk implement -d`
**Checkout plan #8801:** - In current wt: `erk br co --for-plan 8801` - In new wt: `erk br co --new-slot --for-plan 8801`
**Dispatch plan #8801:** - CLI command: `erk pr dispatch 8801` - Slash command: `/erk:pr-dispatch`
session.user: <local-command-caveat>Caveat: The messages below were generated by the user while running local commands. DO NOT respond to these messages or otherwise consider them in your response unless the user explicitly asks you to.</local-command-caveat>
